summary: AGENTS-first governance persistence is complete and independently verified PASS. `AGENTS.md` now defines explicit contract semantics and authority rules; root `agents/**` are authoritative and `.opencode/agents/**` are synced dev projections. Root agent profiles now expose `may_execute`, `may_delegate`, `terminal`, `accept_gate`, `workflow_order`, `verify_gate`, `failure_return`, and `scope_paths`. `hiveminder` is now orchestration-only, `hivefiver` is the bounded framework-writer, and `hivexplorer` / `hiverd` / `hiveq` are terminal non-mutating specialists. Mirrors were synced and `.opencode/agents/hivemaker.md` no longer contains the broken row. One residual contradiction in `hiveq` permissions was fixed and re-verified.
nextSteps: 1) Start fresh session from this handoff. 2) Treat runtime attachment as already bootstrapped and AGENTS/profile persistence as done. 3) Resume the harness/architecture layer: define canonical entry-kernel
and resume/reattach model. 4) Address remaining harness conflicts selectively: role-vs-mode/permission friction
legacy terminology drift between hard runtime concepts and soft profile language
lineage simplification where hivefiver can rely on innate OpenCode workers
and stronger prompt-engineering structure for agent profiles. 5) Only after that proceed into hm-plan / deeper runtime-owned intake work.

packet.scope: Restart handoff after AGENTS-first governance persistence and verification pass, before deeper runtime architecture and hm-harness planning.
constraints: Commands remain prompt contracts only. Deterministic routing must remain in plugins/tools/hooks. Shell is non-interactive/no-TTY. Do not bootstrap HiveMind by manual file writes. Preserve self-host/dev-kit distinction. Root agents are authority; .opencode agents are synced dev projections.
memoryScope: Use this handoff as the first context artifact after restart. Treat AGENTS/profile governance persistence as complete and verified. Resume from runtime-owned intake architecture and harness conflict resolution
successMetrics: 1) New session recognizes AGENTS-first persistence is complete. 2) Next work starts from canonical entry-kernel/intake-record/route-order/resume-reattach design. 3) Follow-up implementation keeps deterministic routing in runtime code
not commands. 4) Any further asset edits use bounded innate OpenCode workers if direct hivefiver delegation is sticky.
packet.returnContract: On resume: summarize that AGENTS-first governance persistence is complete and verified PASS; restate that commands are prompt contracts only and deterministic routing belongs in plugins/tools/hooks; then continue with canonical entry-kernel + intake-record + route-order + resume/reattach design before broader hm-plan/runtime architecture work. If framework-asset edits are needed during that cycle, use innate OpenCode worker agents directly rather than sticky recursive hivefiver delegation.
packet.returnGate: Resume only after acknowledging the AGENTS/profile layer is no longer the active blocker.
packet.resumeTarget: Use this handoff as the first context artifact after restart and continue from harness/runtime architecture design, not from profile governance discovery.
